opp.name: Atlas Air Worldwide S2P
opp.ais_why:
• No discovery call has been recorded in Avoma yet, so customer AI sentiment is unknown.
• Salesforce frames the deal as an Ariba displacement with a strong fit in the aviation vertical; Atlas Air just submitted an RFI on 5/11, indicating a structured buy rather than an AI-driven mandate.
• Activity is process-driven (RFI submitted, 550K ACV, Qualified) without verbatim AI cues to lift it above Curious.

items.quote: Zero matched Avoma calls discovered via opp/account/attendee pulls
0 OpportunityContactRoles on this opp; account-level contacts exist but involvement in S2P deal unconfirmed
No Metrics, EB, Decision Criteria detail, Decision Process, Paper Process, Pain, Champion, or Competition documented in fields or calls
RFI submitted 11 May per Next Step, but RFI questions, our response, and buyer feedback not in Salesforce

metrics.narrative: No business problem, quantified impact, or value case documented in any Salesforce field or call. The product scope (full S2P suite including Merlin AI) implies procurement transformation intent, but no buyer-articulated pain, savings target, cycle-time reduction, or ROI has been captured. Without matched buyer calls and with zero MEDDPICC-related fields populated, the business case remains entirely undefined. The immediate move is to conduct a discovery call that surfaces and quantifies the operational or financial pain Atlas Air is solving for.

sources: Recent task contacts (Director of Procurement, Senior Buyer) not confirmed as champions
champion.narrative: No champion identified or developed. Zero OpportunityContactRoles exist, and recent tasks reference the Director of Procurement and a Senior Buyer (account-level contacts, not confirmed for this opp). No fields (X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Champion_identified__c null) are populated, and no calls show advocacy, access to power, or active deal-driving behavior. In early Qualified stage, champion-building is the foundation—without an internal advocate who has access to the EB/DM and pushes our story, the deal will stall at RFI/shortlist. The immediate move is to identify a procurement or business stakeholder who sees value in the Zycus platform (especially AI/Merlin) and can navigate the committee for us.

competition.narrative: Competition is entirely unknown. The Competitors__c field is null, no other fields (Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c) are populated, and zero calls or tasks name a rival. The RFI submission implies Atlas Air is evaluating multiple vendors (standard for enterprise S2P), but we do not know who else is in the running—likely peers include Coupa, SAP Ariba, Jaggaer, GEP, or incumbent legacy systems. Without competitive intel we cannot tailor our positioning or wedge strategy. The move is to ask directly in the next buyer conversation who else they are considering and what their current state/incumbent looks like, and to debrief the RFI for competitive signals.

identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain has been articulated by the buyer. The product scope (full S2P suite including Merlin AI for intake, sourcing, contract, invoice, procurement, supplier management, and risk) implies broad procurement transformation—likely addressing manual processes, lack of spend visibility, supplier risk, or compliance gaps—but this is inferred from the solution scope, not confirmed buyer pain. No fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Compelling_Event__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c) are populated, and zero calls provide buyer quotes. The RFI may have contained pain statements; we need to review the RFI and validate pain ownership and urgency in a discovery call.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review requirements, InfoSec process, procurement channel, and any SI/partner involvement are all unknown. No fields (Mandate__c, Current_Contract_Expiration__c null), no calls, and no tasks provide visibility. For a large S2P implementation, understanding whether Atlas Air requires MSA redlines, InfoSec questionnaires, ERP integration deep-dives, or procurement-managed SOW/Order Form flow is critical to realistic timeline planning. The close date of March 2027 allows time, but without knowing the paper trail we cannot validate feasibility. This will be uncovered as the deal advances past RFI into shortlist and demo stages.

sources: Account-level contact roster (CFO/CIO present but not linked to opp)
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified or engaged. Account-level contacts include CFO Spencer Schwartz, CIO Joel Goldberg, and procurement directors, but none are confirmed as involved in this opportunity. No EB-related Salesforce fields are populated (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c null), no Executive Sponsor or Economic Buyer contact role exists, and zero calls provide visibility into who controls the commercial decision or budget authority. For a $550k S2P transformation the EB is likely CFO or a senior procurement/supply-chain executive. The move is to map the approval chain and secure direct EB engagement before advancing past Qualified.

sources: Next_Step__c implies procurement-led RFI but no further detail
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is unknown. The RFI submission on 11 May suggests a structured procurement-led evaluation (typical for enterprise S2P), but who reviews responses, the shortlist timeline, the demo/workshop sequence, and the approval chain are all undocumented. No fields (What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c) are populated, and the call history is empty. The stakeholder map is also missing—only account-level contacts exist, none confirmed as involved. Without knowing who participates at each gate and when decisions happen, we cannot plan the path to the relevant stakeholder move is to map the process and milestone dates in the re-engagement call.

sources: Zero matched calls to extract criteria
decision_criteria.narrative: An RFI was submitted on 11 May 2026 per Next Step, which signals the buyer is running a formal evaluation process and has articulated at least preliminary requirements. However, the specific criteria Atlas Air is evaluating against are unknown—no fields (Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c) are populated, and no calls or tasks detail what they prioritize (cost, AI/automation depth, integration with existing ERP/systems, supplier network breadth, ease of use). The RFI response itself is not in the record. The next move is to debrief the RFI internally, understand what Atlas Air asked for, and validate our read of their decision criteria in a follow-up discovery session.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, and AIS_Why__c are all null—no AI excitement documented. The product scope (Merlin across every S2P module) implies the deal team sees AI fit, but the buyer's appetite and understanding are unknown without calls. Need discovery to assess whether Atlas Air is AI Hungry, AI Curious, or needs education.

items.change: Last activity date (no detail on what activity)

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 15 April 2026, RFI submitted 11 May 2026 (~6 weeks ago), and last activity logged 26 May 2026 (31 days ago). No field-history stage progression, no amount changes, no close-date slips. Pulse is COOLING—no verified buyer engagement for a month in early Qualified stage. The RFI submission is the only documented milestone; post-RFI activity (shortlist, demos, follow-up) is absent from the record.

items: RFI submitted 11 May 2026, no subsequent milestone or buyer engagement documented
confidence_signals.summary: Deal is cooling—31 days since last activity, zero buyer calls, RFI submitted 6 weeks ago with no documented follow-up. Early Qualified stage with 259 days to close gives runway, but silence and lack of MEDDPICC visibility are red flags.

north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 259 (close date 2027-03-12). Time in Qualified stage: ~72 days since creation on 15 April 2026. Last verified activity: 26 May 2026 (31 days ago). Next step indicates RFI submission on 11 May, but no subsequent buyer movement documented. Given stage and long runway, cooling pattern warrants re-engagement.
evidence: Next step field shows RFI submitted 11 May 2026
Zero buyer calls discovered via opp/account/attendee pulls
Stage Qualified with 259 days to close is defensible for Pipeline
No contact roles on opportunity, minimal stakeholder visibility
north_star_verdict.headline: RFI submitted 11 May but 31 days of radio silence since last activity (26 May). Qualified stage with 259 days runway allows recovery time, yet zero buyer engagement, no stakeholder calls, and unmapped competition signal a cooling deal needing urgent.

flags: Position Merlin AI as the enterprise transformation wedge—the product scope includes Merlin across intake, sourcing, negotiation, and risk, which is our strongest differentiator against traditional S2P platforms; make the AI/automation story the centerpiece of demos and ROI discussions once we re-engage.
best_practice_check.summary: Early Qualified stage with full S2P+Merlin scope and 259 days to close, but deal is cooling (31 days since last activity). Three levers to raise win probability: (1) re-engage immediately to restart momentum and map the post-RFI process, (2) multi-thread beyond a single procurement contact to build champion and reach power (EB/DM), and (3) position Merlin AI as the transformation differentiator given the comprehensive product scope that signals AI appetite.

competitive_position.summary: Competition is entirely unknown. No competitors named in any Salesforce field, call, or task. The RFI process implies a multi-vendor evaluation (standard enterprise S2P procurement), but the shortlist, incumbent, and peer rivals are undocumented. Without intel, we cannot assess our standing or craft a differentiated wedge. Once the competitive landscape is mapped, the S2P suite breadth plus Merlin AI (negotiation agent, intake automation, risk scoring) will be the primary differentiators against traditional platforms.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Product scope includes Merlin AI across intake, sourcing, contracts, invoicing, procurement, supplier, and risk—a comprehensive AI footprint that signals transformation ambition. However, AIS fields are null, and zero calls mean we have no visibility into whether the AI story is resonating or even being told. The full Merlin suite is a strong differentiator for enterprise S2P; we must make it the narrative centerpiece once we re-engage.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Product scope is comprehensive (full S2P suite including Merlin AI across intake, sourcing, contracts, invoicing, procurement, supplier, and risk), signaling Atlas Air's appetite for end-to-end transformation. However, their specific evaluation criteria, pain points, and fit against our capabilities are undocumented. The RFI may have revealed expectations; we need to review it and validate fit in discovery.

• Activity is process-driven (RFI submitted, 550K ACV, Qualified) without verbatim AI cues to lift it above Curious.
